channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 food service employees killed in FSU shooting
item.description: A food service worker was identified as one of two people killed when a gunman opened fire on the Florida State University campus Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KTLA 5

item.title: FSU shooting victims include a school employee whose dad was a Cuban exile turned CIA operative
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Two people were killed and six others were injured when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University, sending students fleeing from the student union and putting the Tallahassee, Florida, campus under lockdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Venice expands day-tripper tax in bid to combat overtourism
item.description: VENICE, Italy (AP) — Venice is charging day-trippers to the famed canal city an arrivals tax for the second year starting Friday, a measure aimed at combating the kind of overtourism that put the city's UNESCO World Cultural Heritage status at risk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: College spring breaker charged in firebombing of Tesla Cybertrucks at dealership
item.description: A Boston college student faces federal charges for an alleged arson at a Tesla dealership in Kansas City, Missouri, while on spring break.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical drinks by the pool? Not so fast, says senator who visited Abrego Garcia in El Salvador
item.description: There was the pool furniture in the background. There were the tropical drinks, which looked to be margaritas garnished with cherries. And then there were the deported prisoner and the American senator, sitting and chatting.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 10,000 pages of records about Robert F. Kennedy's 1968 assassination are released, on Trump's order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About 10,000 pages of records related to the 1968 assassination of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy were released Friday, including handwritten notes by the gunman, who said the Democratic presidential candidate “must be disposed of” and acknowledged an obsession with killing him.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and HAYA PANJWANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ACLU asks Supreme Court to block ‘imminent’ wave of Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) asked the Supreme Court on Friday to immediately intervene to block the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants being held in Texas to El Salvador under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Abrego Garcia told visiting US senator he was no longer being held at notorious Salvadoran prison
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen was flying home from El Salvador on Friday after meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported there by the Trump administration. It’s unclear what will happen next in the case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Join these viewing events to see bighorn sheep in their natural habitat
item.description: If you've ever wanted to catch a glimpse of bighorn sheep in the wild, you may want to check out these two viewing events hosted by the Utah Division of Wildlife Resources.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: PICTURES: Volunteers show up to groom, treat 156 animals rescued in Ogden
item.description: OGDEN, Utah (ABC4) — Volunteers showed up at the Weber County Animal Service shelter on Friday to bathe, deworm and treat over 100 animals rescued earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Kade Garner, ABC 4

item.title: Eagle Mountain celebrates Easter with massive 70,000 egg hunt
item.description: Eagle Mountain citizens were quite festive for the Easter holiday, thousands of families showing up for the city's annual Easter egg hunt — complete with an impressive 70,000 eggs.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks CFPB layoffs, probes potential violation of order
item.description: A federal judge temporarily barred the Trump administration from laying off roughly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) as she mulls whether it violates her previous order. At a Friday hearing, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Military officials identify 2 Marines killed in crash
item.description: Two Camp Pendleton Marines killed in a crash during a convoy movement in New Mexico Tuesday have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's claims about Abrego Garcia’s gang ties largely rely on 1 confidential tip
item.description: The Trump administration’s argument that the mistakenly deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia is a member of MS-13 is based largely on a confidential tip he and his family have long disputed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Menendez brothers attorney's focus on grisly photos part of strategic defense play to free killers: experts
item.description: Lyle and Erik Menendez will return to court on May 9 in their bid for resentencing, as legal experts break down the defense's strategy to reduce the killer brothers' life sentences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The NFL Draft is one week away; 49ers, Raiders eye top-valued picks
item.description: The 2025 NFL Draft begins on April 24 and the San Francisco 49ers and the Las Vegas Raiders both have a good shot at snagging top-10 valued talent.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump officials' defiance over Abrego Garcia's deportation is 'shocking,' appeals court says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration’s claim that it can't do anything to free Kilmar Abrego Garcia from an El Salvador prison and return him to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FSU lecturer finds broken doors in building after 2-hour shooting lockdown
item.description: Busted doors and debris were what a lecturer at Florida State University found after being locked down for hours during Thursday's mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Pizookie coming to BJ’s Restaurants
item.description: The Broccoli Cheddar Pizookie, slated to debut at BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse nationwide, won’t be coming to restaurants after all. Instead, BJ’s will be releasing a Snickers Pizookie. “We've come to our senses.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KRON4

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, KRON4

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died of complications from diabetes, says NYC medical examiner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died as a result of complications from diabetes, New York City's medical examiner said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Slave descendants fight to protect their threatened island community at Georgia's highest court
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Georgia's highest court waded Wednesday into a fight between Black landowners and local officials who have weakened long-standing protections for one of the South's last Gullah-Geechee communities founded by freed slaves.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harvard's challenge to Trump administration could test limits of government power
item.description: On one side is Harvard, the nation’s oldest and wealthiest university, with a brand so powerful that its name is synonymous with prestige. On the other side is the Trump administration, determined to go further than any other White House to reshape American higher education.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: The arson attack at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s (D) residence is the latest incident to underscore the threats faced by high-profile politicians amid the country’s political polarization.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9
